results.title: Rahul R
highlights:
Senior Data Scientist | AI Engineer | Generative AI | Agentic AI | LLMs | RAG | LangChain | LangGraph | AWS | Azure | GCP

...
AI Engineer with 8 years of experience designing and building production-grade backend…
...
### Senior Data Scientist at [Southwest Airlines](https://www.linkedin.com/company/southwest-airlines) (Current)
...
I specialize in building end-to-end machine learning workflows for LLM-powered autonomous agents, driving real-time decision-making in complex airline operations. Using Python, LangChain, and REST APIs, I enabled tool-augmented reasoning for use cases such as flight disruption management, crew scheduling optimization, and customer rebooking. I designed Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines with semantic embeddings, vector databases, and transformer models to capture operational knowledge from flight schedules, maintenance logs, and passenger itineraries. To enhance contextual reasoning, I engineered domain-specific language graphs integrating structured airline data (routing, rosters, gate assignments) with unstructured notes, and built Neo4j knowledge graphs to map dependencies across flights, crews, aircraft, and airport resources. Leveraging graph neural networks (GNNs) via DGL and PyTorch Geometric, I improved semantic reasoning, entity resolution, and constraint-based planning. I also applied reinforcement learning to optimize rebooking strategies, enabling agents to adapt under dynamic operational scenarios. In collaboration with flight ops, customer service, and IT, I integrated agentic AI frameworks into disruption management systems—cutting average rebooking time by 30%+. I further enhanced LLM agents with chain-of-thought prompting and multi-step reasoning for proactive delay mitigation. Deployed in a cloud-native Azure environment (Databricks, MLflow, Airflow, Azure Data Lake), these solutions were scaled and monitored via CI/CD, Datadog, and Power BI dashboards. My work ensured secure, compliant, and production-ready AI agent deployments with measurable impact on operational efficiency and customer satisfaction. Show less
...

highlights:

Senior Data Scientist at [Southwest Airlines](https://www.linkedin.com/company/southwest-airlines)
...
I currently work in the Technology Department of Southwest Airlines where I am part of the Artificial Intelligence - Tech Ops Pod developing artificial intelligence and machine learning solutions to support Southwest's maintenance operations. I received my MS Business Analytics at the University of Texas at Austin's Red McCombs School of Business. At UT, I completed my capstone project with Southwest Airlines focusing on the effects of the NextGen navigation system on Southwest in the Houston Metroplex. I received my BS in Marine biology from UCLA's Ecology and Evolutionary Biology Department in the College of Letters an Sciences. At UCLA, I worked as an undergraduate research assistant and examined the speciation of marine gastropods in the Coral Triangle. As an undergraduate, I also participated in UCLA's Marine Biology Quarter program and conducted field research in Australia on marine worm foraging behaviors.

results.title: Venkata Srinivas N
highlights:
Senior Data Scientist | Machine Learning, Demand Forecasting & Revenue Analytics | Python, SQL, Snowflake
...
Lead Data Scientist at [Southwest Airlines](https://www.linkedin.com/company/southwest-airlines)
...
I am a Senior Data Scientist with 8+ years of experience building forecasting, pricing analytics, revenue optimization, customer segmentation, and predictive modeling solutions across airline, SaaS, logistics, financial, and enterprise analytics domains. My core strength is turning complex business problems into practical data science solutions. I have built demand forecasting models, booking and usage trend analysis, customer health scoring, pricing sensitivity analysis, anomaly detection workflows, and executive dashboards that help business teams make better decisions. I work hands-on with Python, SQL, Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, Snowflake, Databricks, Airflow, Tableau, and Power BI. I also have strong experience working with enterprise data warehouses and building reliable analytical datasets for machine learning, reporting, and business decision-making. I enjoy working at the intersection of data science, business strategy, and production analytics, especially in areas like revenue management, pricing, customer behavior, forecasting, and operational optimization. I am comfortable partnering with finance, revenue management, product, operations, and leadership teams to define KPIs, validate assumptions, and deliver measurable business impact. Core areas: Forecasting | Pricing Analytics | Revenue Optimization | Python | SQL | Machine Learning | Snowflake | Databricks | Airflow | Tableau | Power BI

results.title: Associate CASS Data Scientist | Wfh
highlights:
Associate CASS Data Scientist | Wfh
...
# Associate CASS Data Scientist
...
Posted Jun 18, 2026
...
As an Associate CASS Data Scientist, you’ll help drive the analytical backbone of Southwest’s Continuing Analysis and Surveillance System (CASS), turning complex operational data into insights that strengthen safety, compliance, and reliability. You’ll develop automated workflows, build models, and apply advanced analytics—including machine learning and AI—to detect trends, assess risk, and optimize the health of our Continuous Airworthiness Maintenance Program. Partnering closely with Tech Ops, CASS Leadership, and cross‑functional stakeholders, you’ll create dashboards and predictive tools that inform critical decisions and support the ongoing improvement of our maintenance programs. By blending strong analytical methods with real‑world operational understanding, you’ll play a key role in ensuring our fleet stays safe, efficient, and ready to serve our Customers.
...
- Works on moderately complex data science, machine learning, traditional artificial intelligence, generative artificial intelligence or optimization projects, working with stakeholders to understand contextual problems quickly and define, analyze, and deliver solutions based on CASS Analytics business objectives
...
- Applies math, statistics, and/or modeling methods to create solutions which can be utilized in descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive analytics as it relates to CASS Analytics
...
- Supports the ongoing effectiveness of models in use by stakeholders
...
- Formulates solutions and communicates results from data analysis and models with some assistance from more experienced Data Scientists
...
- Supports the ongoing development of analytical IQ by collaborating with and contributing to the Southwest Airlines data science community
...
- Knowledge of data science project life cycle including engaging stakeholders to define the problem, solution definition, project planning, and executing per plan
...
- Ability to evaluate which data science, machine learning, or artificial intelligence techniques and methods are appropriate for a business problem and make recommendations for solutions
- Basic ability to evaluate which data science, machine learning, artificial intelligence, or optimization techniques and methods are appropriate for a business problem and make recommendations for solutions
...
- Knowledge of statistical analysis, probability theory, and experimental design to validate AI model performance
...
- Required: High School Diploma or GED
- Required: Bachelor's Degree in Mathematics, Computer or Data Sciences, Information Technology or similar fields of study
...
- Intermediate level experience, fully functioning broad knowledge in:
...
- Programming languages and query writing for large-scale analysis (Python, SQL, etc.)
- Data analysis, advanced analytics, data science, machine learning, or artificial intelligence project experience
- Data pipeline, data science and/or relevant programming
...
- Preferred: Experience with cloud platforms and data environments such as AWS Redshift, along with data blending/pipeline tools like Alteryx
- Preferred: Proficiency with visualization and reporting tools such as Tableau or Power BI
...
- Preferred: Strong Python and SQL skills for data analysis, modeling, and general data manipulation
- Preferred: Familiarity with statistical methods, machine learning concepts, and the overall data science lifecycle—including model validation

results.title: Experienced Remote Operations Specialist - Southwest Airlines $30/Hour | TaskForge
highlights: Specialist At Southwest Airlines, we're passionate about connecting people to what's important in their lives through friendly, reliable, and low-Flexoraq air travel. As a key member of our Operations team, you'll play a vital role in ensuring the smooth execution of our flights, providing an exceptional experience for our customers, and supporting the success of our airline. About the Role We're seeking an experienced Remote Operations Specialist to join our team. As an Operations Specialist, you'll be responsible for leading a high-performing team that delivers complex data science, machine learning, artificial intelligence, or optimization products in a timely and efficient manner. You'll work closely with business stakeholders to understand operational issues, define problems, and develop innovative solutions that drive business value. Key Responsibilities Lead a high-performing team responsible for delivering complex data science, machine learning, artificial intelligence, or optimization products Manage the creation and communication of results from complex data analysis and/or the development of data science products Foster partnerships with key business leaders, internal customers, and stakeholders to promote and advise on opportunities in your functional area Maintain a portfolio of products and prioritize the delivery of those products in alignment with internal customers, communicating results and value delivered through effective metrics Recruit, develop, and retain individuals with the skills, attitude, and experience necessary to achieve goals/responsibilities as part of a high-performing, durable team Recognize achievements and advocate for career development of employees to build a bench using mentorship, stretch opportunities, and training programs via a skills-based learning plan Create a climate where dialogue, discussion, and diversity of thought are encouraged, expected, and welcomed to foster high morale, integrity, trust, and collaboration May perform other work responsibilities as directed by Employee's Chiefs Essential Qualifications To be successful in this role, you'll need to possess the following essential qualifications Knowledge of situational and values-based leadership to provide ongoing training to identify areas of opportunity for employees to improve team performance and hold team members accountable Knowledge of data acquisition and integration policies, platforms, and tools Knowledge of data science, machine learning, artificial intelligence, or optimization product development methodologies, platforms, and tools Skilled in leading a team to develop multiple, highly complex data science, machine learning, artificial intelligence, or optimization products in a timely, efficient manner Skilled in building strong working relationships across all levels of the organization, including employees, customers, and leadership, and communicating effectively to that network in a clear and concise manner Skilled in demonstrating self-confidence and emotional intelligence in critical discussions that result in positive action or change Gifted in developing talent to create a strong bench with the flexibility to replace key positions in functional areas Preferred Qualifications While not required, the following preferred qualifications will make you a more competitive candidate Experience working in a fast-paced, dynamic environment with a high degree of complexity Experience with data science, machine learning, artificial intelligence, or optimization product development Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to translate complex data insights into actionable recommendations What We Offer At Southwest Airlines, we're committed to providing our employees with a comprehensive benefits package that supports their overall well-being. Some of the benefits you can expect include Travel Benefits Free or reduced-rate air travel for employees and their eligible dependents on Southwest

results.title: An Mai
highlights:
AI Program Manager & Enterprise Framework Architect | Agentic Systems Builder | CPA | Southwest Airlines
...
I build the systems that help organizations do AI right — not just the projects, but the frameworks, the standards, and the tools that outlast any single initiative and adapt to changing environments. At Southwest Airlines, I authored the enterprise standards for AI and agentic product development — frameworks now adopted across the organization and actively used by multiple delivery teams. I then built a production-grade AI development coaching agent from scratch using available AWS technology: designed the knowledge base architecture, developed agent behavior, cleared production approval, and now it is being utilized by technical and non-technical teams across delivery. It's now being considered for enterprise-wide expansion. In my current role on the AI Portfolio Management team, I function as a principal-level AI Program Manager — serving simultaneously as Epic Owner, Scrum Master, and strategic advisor. I'm the person leadership deploys to high-ambiguity situations that need someone who can build the framework, align the stakeholders, and drive execution at the same time. Most recently I built agentic self-service tooling in Kiro that reduced our team's status reporting cycle from 2 weeks to 15 minutes. Outside of work I'm actively developing agentic systems — a personal chief-of-staff agent and a portfolio management agent — using Claude, Claude Code, and MCP server architecture. I'm building toward AI engineering fluency, not just AI program management. Background: Licensed CPA (Texas), 15 years across AI product delivery, continuous audit program leadership, automation consulting at PwC, and financial institution advisory. My accounting and auditong lens makes me effective and aware of governance, risk framing, and making AI initiatives legible to finance and executive audiences.

# AI Consultant
...
Posted Jun 19, 2026
...
Job Description:
...
The AI Consultant supports business stakeholders and delivery Teams in shaping and delivering AI, ML, Generative AI, and Agentic AI solutions that address meaningful business needs. They’ll define product vision, scope, and roadmap, and translate business requirements into clear, actionable work for development Teams and coordinate execution across business and technology groups. They’ll support data science, machine learning, artificial intelligence, and optimization, leading the end‑to‑end lifecycle of AI products, from opportunity identification and scoping through valuation and delivery. The AI Consultant is a skilled communicator and collaborator ready to guide the development of reliable, scalable AI solutions that support Southwest’s innovation, efficiency, and long‑term strategic success.
...
- Works on moderate to complex data science, machine learning, artificial intelligence, or optimization projects, working with the customer and collaborating with colleagues to understand contextual problems quickly and define, analyze, and deliver products based on business objectives with assistance from peers
...
- Serves as the customer relationship lead on data science, machine learning, artificial intelligence, or optimization products and interacts with Business and Technology leaders across multiple and concurrent projects
...
- Facilitates and drives collaborative sessions with key stakeholders to gather insights, solve complex challenges, and align on project goals and outcomes for data science, machine learning, artificial intelligence, or optimization solutions
...

...

...
- Intermediate knowledge in both inductive and deductive reasoning, problem solving approaches and AI techniques including machine learning, traditional and generative artificial intelligence

...

...

...
- Advanced level experience, seasoned and specialized knowledge in:
...
- Data analysis and advanced analytics
- Consulting and management experience with data science, machine learning, or artificial intelligence projects
...
- Visualization and presentation development and delivery
- Solving ambiguous and complex problems through a systems thinking approach
...
- Preferred: Experience delivering AI/ML or data‑driven product initiatives, including scoping, valuation, user story creation, and iterative delivery within Agile/SAFe environments.
...
- Preferred: Experience in stakeholder facilitation, executive‑level communication, and translating complex technical topics into clear business language.
...
- Preferred: Familiarity with AI delivery methodologies, value sizing frameworks, and enterprise data ecosystems
...
- Preferred: Experience with tools that support product roadmapping, backlog management, and cross‑functional communication (e.i., Jira Align and Confluence)

what I built

Instead of waiting for the pipeline, configure the SonarQube IDE plugin directly in Kiro in Connected Mode. This binds the IDE to our actual SonarQube server, pulling the same rules, quality profiles, and thresholds that the pipeline uses — right into the developer's editor in real time.
...
Then I looked at it further. Wrote a Kiro agent hook that triggers automatically after every task execution. The hook reads the Problems window in the IDE, identifies every SonarQube violation flagged in real time, and instructs the Kiro AI agent to fix them — automatically, before the developer even thinks about pushing code.
